description: Building and maintaining high-traffic C#/.NET APIs at scale, with ownership spanning design, deployment, and production reliability. Took the lead on delivering a greenfield API service end-to-end, and contributed to improving the team's testing and CI/CD practices. Work spans both backend systems and full-stack internal tooling.

position: Foundations of Software Engineering Teaching Assistant
description: Supported students across full-stack development topics including frontend and backend principles, software design, and engineering best practices. Led lab sessions, graded assignments with constructive feedback, and held office hours for individualized guidance.

Built a full-stack internal tool using .NET Core and React TypeScript for real-time microservices monitoring and notifications. Deployed the application via IIS onto a Windows server and implemented a persistent Windows service for dynamic polling and updates.

Designed and developed a Unity game in C# for a research study on game-based handwriting skill improvement. Integrated real-time handwriting data capture and analysis into the game, and conducted a study with 15+ participants as part of an undergraduate research thesis.
Modernized legacy COBOL interfaces by rebuilding them using .NET WinForms and Visual Basic, improving usability and database interaction efficiency.
